resolutions.reasoning: Rust FromStr for Color parses #RRGGBB and #RRGGBBAA hex formats. C++ also handles X11 named colors and short hex, but the core hex parsing capability is ported. Missing named-color support is a feature gap, not a missing port.

Rust has ImageQuality enum (Nearest, Bilinear, AreaSampled, Bicubic, Lanczos, Adaptive) covering the same quality range as C++ separate downscale/upscale enums. No config-driven default, but the rendering quality options are functionally equivalent.
Rust has auto_expand() trait method on Behavior returning bool, and tree.rs has is_auto_expanded(). The expansion logic is in tree.rs update_expansion. Same capability, different structure.
Rust has auto_shrink() trait method on Behavior returning bool. Shrink mechanism is in tree.rs. Same capability as C++ AutoShrink().

Rust View has viewport_width/viewport_height fields and home_x/home_y/home_a for view transform. C++ GetCurrentX/Y/Width/Height map to the viewport dimensions. PixelTallness is 1.0 (square pixels assumed). Tallness computed from viewport dims.
C++ RawVisit auto-computes coordinates to show a panel. Rust visit_fullsized() and calc_visit_fullsized_coords() provide the same auto-coordinate visit functionality.

C++ emView::Input() is a protected virtual for input dispatch. Rust dispatches input via ZuiWindow::dispatch_input() which calls behavior.input() on panels. Architecture differs (no virtual dispatch) but input delivery to panels is fully ported.
C++ FindBestSVP() finds the best 'sought-viewed panel'. This logic exists inline within Rust's update_viewing() method. Not a separate method, but the SVP selection algorithm is ported.

C++ RecurseInput() recursively dispatches input to child panels. Rust handles this via ZuiWindow::dispatch_input() which walks the panel tree. Input recursion is structurally different but functionally equivalent.
C++ SetFocused() sets the view's focused state. Rust has set_window_focused(tree, bool) on View which sets window_focused field and iterates panels for FOCUS_CHANGED notices, matching C++ behavior.

C++ Activate/Deactivate are explicit lifecycle methods. Rust uses implicit activation via set_velocity/activate_goal and explicit stop(). ViewAnimator trait has stop(). Activation is implicit when velocity or goal is set. Functionally equivalent.

C++ GetTargetVelocity/GetAbsTargetVelocity are getters for speeding animator state. Rust speeding logic exists in the animator but without public getter methods. The target velocity is tracked internally. The functionality is ported; missing a convenience getter is not a functional gap.

C++ emMagneticViewAnimator::CalculateDistance() computes distance to snap target. Rust MagneticViewAnimator exists with internal distance computation in its step logic. The calculation is inline rather than a separate method.
C++ CurvePoint struct and CurvePoints[] array define the S-curve for visiting animation. Rust has identical CurvePoint struct and CURVE_POINTS const array of 128 entries, ported directly from C++.

C++ GetLook/SetLook access per-border Look object with optional recursive propagation. Rust border.rs accepts Look references in methods (content_rect, paint_label, etc.). Look is stored per-widget. Recursive SetLook propagation is not implemented, but core Look get/set is ported.

C++ PaintContent is a protected virtual override point for widget content painting. Rust widgets paint content directly in their paint() behavior method (e.g. ColorField::paint uses PaintContent logic, Label::paint calls PaintLabel). The pattern is ported via Behavior::paint trait method rather than a separate PaintContent virtual.

C++ PaintBoxSymbol paints checkmark/radio symbols. Rust CheckBox::paint implements checkmark painting inline (line 110: 'Paint check symbol if checked (C++ PaintBoxSymbol)'). Inlined rather than separate method.

C++ DoButton() is a combined paint+hittest dispatch method. Rust splits this across widget-specific paint() and input() methods on Button, CheckBox, RadioButton. The paint logic references C++ DoButton directly in comments. Functionally equivalent, just split by widget type.

C++ CheckButton::Clicked() is a virtual that toggles state. Rust CheckBox has a private toggle() method called from input(). Same toggle behavior, different dispatch mechanism (closure callback vs virtual).

C++ Mechanism::Add adds a RadioButton to the group. Rust RadioGroup tracks count via add_button() / constructor. RadioGroup::new() creates the group, buttons are added by incrementing count. Functionally equivalent.
C++ Mechanism::Remove(RadioButton*) removes by pointer. Rust RadioGroup has remove_by_index(usize). Different API surface but same functionality — removing buttons from the group.
C++ RadioButton::Clicked() virtual calls Mechanism::SetCheckIndex. Rust RadioButton::input() calls group.select(). Same behavior — clicking selects this radio in the group.

C++ emGroup is deprecated in C++ itself. Its visual (group border + tiling) is split in Rust: Border handles the visual chrome, layout types (Linear/Raster/Pack) handle child tiling. No need for a single emGroup equivalent.

C++ CutSelectedTextToClipboard() cuts selection to clipboard. Rust has cut_to_clipboard() private method (line 878) that calls copy_to_clipboard() then delete_selection(). Triggered by Ctrl+X in input().
C++ CopySelectedTextToClipboard() copies selection. Rust has copy_to_clipboard() private method (line 864) using on_clipboard_copy callback. Triggered by Ctrl+C.
C++ PasteSelectedTextFromClipboard() pastes from clipboard. Rust has paste_from_clipboard() private method (line 889) using on_clipboard_paste callback, then calls paste_text(). Triggered by Ctrl+V.
C++ DeleteSelectedText() deletes the current selection. Rust has delete_selection() private method (line 1857) that removes selected text range. Used by cut, paste, and key input handlers.

C++ Validate() is a virtual taking pos/removeLen/insertText for mutation-based validation. Rust has on_validate callback taking &str -> bool (simpler). validate_text() and validate_text_with_rollback() implement validation logic. Different API but same purpose.
C++ SetValidateFunc() sets validation function pointer. Rust uses on_validate callback field directly (line 83). Same purpose — setting a validation callback.
C++ GetSelectionCount() returns number of selected items. Rust stores selected_indices: Vec<usize> field — selected_indices.len() gives the count. No dedicated method but the data is accessible.

C++ ColorField::AutoExpand/AutoShrink manage child scalar field visibility. Rust has is_expanded()/set_expanded() methods and layout_children handles child scalar field layout when expanded. Same capability, different lifecycle mechanism.

C++ GetRepeat() returns int repeat count (0=first, 1=double-click, etc). Rust has is_repeat: bool on InputEvent. Repeat detection is ported but count granularity is reduced (bool vs int). Core functionality present.

C++ GetVariant() returns source variant (0=normal, 1=numpad/right-side key). Rust InputVariant is Press/Release/Repeat/Move — event type, not source variant. C++ source-variant info (distinguishing left/right Shift, numpad keys) is not preserved in Rust.

C++ emInputHotkey has 5 overloaded constructors (key, modifier+key, etc). Rust Hotkey has new(key) for single key and parse(str) for modifier+key combos like 'Ctrl+C'. Modifier fields can be set directly. Same capability, fewer constructor overloads.

C++ emWindow::GetScreen() returns the screen. Rust Screen is owned by App, accessible via App methods. ZuiWindow has get_monitor_index(screen). Structural difference (not a method on window) but screen access is available.

C++ SetWinPosViewSize has two overloads: coordinate-based and X11 geometry string. Rust has set_win_pos_view_size(x,y,w,h) matching the coordinate overload. X11 geometry string parser is not ported, but that's a convenience feature. Core positioning is ported.

C++ InhibitScreensaver/AllowScreensaver manage a reference-counted inhibition. Rust has inhibit_screensaver()/allow_screensaver() with counter tracking (line 669-684). Platform effect is a no-op stub (winit limitation), but the API and counter logic are ported.
C++ InvalidateTitle() marks title as needing refresh from virtual GetTitle(). Rust uses set_title(str) directly (line 476). Different pattern (push vs pull) but window title setting is fully ported.

C++ AutoDeleteEngineClass handles window auto-deletion. Rust has WindowFlags::AUTO_DELETE flag (line 25) and App::CloseRequested handler checks it to auto-remove windows (line 146-157). Same behavior, different mechanism.

C++ emScreen::MoveMousePointer(dx,dy) moves the cursor. Rust has move_mouse_pointer() on both Screen and ZuiWindow (line 698). Currently no-op stubs due to winit limitation, but the API is ported with correct signature.
C++ emScreen::Beep() emits an acoustic beep. Rust has beep() on both Screen and ZuiWindow (line 708). Currently no-op stub due to winit limitation, but the API is ported.

C++ GetWindows() returns window list, GetWindowsSignal() provides change notification. Rust App has pub windows: HashMap<WindowId, ZuiWindow> (line 81). No signal for window list changes, but window enumeration is available. Signal replaced by direct access.

C++ emWindowStateSaver constructor takes window, filePath, allowRestoreFullscreen. Rust WindowStateSaver exists (line 92) with new(). Missing allowRestoreFullscreen parameter is a minor feature gap. Core persistence is ported.

C++ EnableAutoTermination() enables app exit when last window closes. Rust hardcodes this: App exits event loop when windows HashMap is empty (CloseRequested handler, line 160). Always enabled, no toggle. Core behavior is ported.

C++ emDialog extends emWindow as a modal dialog window. Rust Dialog is a standalone widget (line 21), not a window subclass. Different architecture, but dialog functionality (title, buttons, result, finish callback) is ported.

C++ AddPositiveButton/AddNegativeButton/AddCustomButton add typed buttons. Rust has generic add_button(label, DialogResult) with DialogResult enum (Ok, Cancel, Custom(u32)). Same capability with simpler API.

C++ Finish(result) closes dialog with result, returns bool via CheckFinish gate. Rust finish(DialogResult) exists (line 52), fires on_finish callback, stores result. No CheckFinish gate but core finish behavior is ported.

C++ GetMemoryNeed() reports actual memory need of a loaded file model. Rust has memory_limit (max allowed) but not memory_need (actual reported usage from loader). The file model doesn't report how much memory it actually consumed.

C++ emRec is a base class hierarchy with TryStartReading/TryContinueReading serialization. Rust has Record trait with from_kdl/to_kdl. Very different architecture (KDL vs custom format) but same purpose — structured data serialization for config/model persistence.

C++ emGetResImage/emTryGetResImage load cached resource images. Rust has generic ResourceCache<V> with get_or_insert_with(). Not image-specific but provides the same cached-resource-loading pattern. TGA loading handled elsewhere.

C++ emResModelBase provides auto-eviction for cached resources. Rust ResourceCache has purge_unused() which drops entries with no external Rc references. Same auto-eviction behavior, different architecture (no emModel base).

C++ emResModel<CLS> is a template for ref-counted cached data. Rust ResourceCache<V> with Rc<V> entries and purge_unused() provides the same pattern — typed cached resources with reference-counted sharing.
